full_description:
I need a robust, search-driven platform that pulls live inventory from independent car-dealer sites—much like [login to view URL]—so shoppers can browse every dealer’s stock in one place and then click straight through to the dealer’s own site to purchase.

Core build 
• Laravel (or another modern PHP framework if you can justify it) will drive the backend. 
• Data connectors must ingest XML, JSON and other open API feeds, normalise the fields, and keep each dealer’s stock in sync automatically. 
• A lightning-fast search layer—Elasticsearch, Algolia or similar—should let users filter by make, model, price, mileage, fuel type and more in milliseconds. 
• Every vehicle detail page needs clean, SEO-optimised URLs and metadata so listings rank individually. 
• The entire front end must be mobile-first and responsive; Vue, React or plain Blade components are all acceptable if performance stays high. 
• Architecture should be container-ready (Docker/Kubernetes) so we can scale horizontally as dealers or traffic grow.

Dealer portal 
Dealers log in to a simple panel where they can: 
- see feed status and error logs 
- enable/disable vehicles manually 
- set default mark-ups or promotional tags. 

(If you have ideas for deeper analytics or user roles, let’s discuss.)

Scope of work / deliverables 
1. Feed integration module with scheduling and real-time webhook support 
2. Search API plus responsive UI components 
3. Dealer management panel with auth and basic analytics 
4. SEO vehicle page templates 
5. Deployment scripts and concise technical documentation

Please include in your bid: 
• links to any automotive, marketplace or data-aggregation projects you have shipped 
• your recommended tech stack (DB, search engine, hosting) 
• ballpark timeline with milestone breakdown and cost estimate

Prior experience with automotive feeds or marketplace aggregation will put you to the top of my shortlist. I’m ready to start as soon as we finalise the stack.

full_description:
I’m assembling a small remote team and need two professionals who are currently based in India or Pakistan.

First, a licensed general practitioner who can work online. Your role will centre on patient-facing consultation and diagnosis, offering clear health advice and education, and drafting concise medical reports that a non-medical audience can understand. You should already be comfortable with basic telemedicine platforms, be able to reference current Indian or Pakistani clinical guidelines where relevant, and produce documentation in clean, well-formatted English.

Second, I’m looking for a financial planning and advisory specialist. I’ll share client scenarios that require goal-based plans, cash-flow projections, and strategic investment suggestions that fit local regulations. Proficiency with Excel (or Google Sheets) for modelling is essential; familiarity with Indian and Pakistani tax-advantaged instruments is a plus, though deep tax preparation isn’t part of this brief.

Deliverables 
• GP: 1) virtual consultation notes, 2) a short educational brief, 3) a formal medical report (PDF or Word). 
• Finance pro: 1) a personalised financial plan document, 2) an advisory call summary, 3) a follow-up action checklist.

Each assignment will be issued separately with clear timelines, and collaboration will take place over Zoom, Google Meet, and shared folders on Google Drive. Accuracy, confidentiality, and prompt turnaround are critical. If you meet the criteria and can start soon, let’s talk.

Fluency in spoken and written English is therefore essential for quick hand-offs with our US team. I also expect strict HIPAA compliance and the ability to track productivity and denial trends.

Deliverables 
• Code and abstract each encounter with 95 %+ accuracy 
• Submit claims through AdvancedMD the same day the chart is locked 
• Post payments/denials and escalate issues immediately 
• End-of-week report summarizing volume, first-pass acceptance rate, and any outstanding appeals

Acceptance criteria 
• Random audit shows ≤ 5 % error rate across ICD-10, CPT, HCPCS codes 
• First-pass claim acceptance ≥ 98 % in AdvancedMD 
• All communication conducted in clear, professional English 

If you already work comfortably in AdvancedMD and can match these targets from the Philippines, I’m ready to start as soon as possible.

full_description:
I need a complete, up-to-date dump of every crafting recipe that lives on the Dungeon Crusher server. The game sits on my Android phone, Steam and the official web client, but none of those surfaces expose the full database; the data has to be pulled directly from the server or the traffic it exchanges with the clients. Fan wikis and other public-facing spreadsheets are not acceptable because they miss newer combinations.

Your task is to locate the authoritative recipe source, extract it in full and hand it back to me as a machine-readable file—TXT, CSV or JSON is fine—where each row maps the exact ingredient combination IDs to the resulting item ID exactly as the server stores them.

Acceptance criteria: 
• 100 % coverage of every current recipe in the live game 
• IDs match the server’s internal naming, not human-friendly labels 
• One line per recipe, clearly separated fields, delivered in TXT/CSV/JSON 
• Date/time of extraction included so I know when the snapshot was taken 

Please outline your technical approach (packet capture, API discovery, decompiling, etc.) and be ready to show a small sample recipe as proof of access before the project is awarded. Familiarity with tools such as Wireshark, Fiddler, Burp Suite or UE asset extractors will likely help you move quickly.

full_description:
We are looking for a highly creative and detail-oriented freelancer to help build a premium printable kids activity membership platform focused on South African children.

The project involves creating beautifully designed weekly educational activity packs for children aged 5–9 (Grade R to Grade 3), aligned with the South African CAPS curriculum.

This is NOT a basic Canva job.

We are looking for someone who:

* Has strong graphic design skills
* Understands children’s educational workbook design
* Can use AI tools effectively
* Has excellent prompt engineering ability
* Can produce consistent high-quality printable pages
* Can follow branding and character consistency
* Has experience designing engaging educational content for kids

⸻

Project Overview

You will help generate and design:

* Weekly printable activity books
* Educational worksheets
* Puzzle pages
* Hidden object activities
* Dot-to-dot activities
* Matching games
* Colouring pages
* Reward charts
* Certificates
* Parent guides
* South African discovery pages
* Language learning pages

Each weekly pack contains approximately:

* 16 A4 portrait pages
* Multiple age variations
* Consistent visual branding
* High-resolution print-ready artwork

⸻

Ages / Grades

The packs will be created for:

* Age 5 (Grade R)
* Age 6 (Grade 1)
* Age 7 (Grade 2)
* Age 8 (Grade 3)
* Age 9 (Grade 4)

⸻

Important Requirements

The successful freelancer MUST:

* Be highly skilled in AI image generation
* Understand how to generate consistent character designs
* Be able to maintain the same look-and-feel across pages
* Create professional print-ready layouts
* Understand typography for children
* Create clean educational worksheet layouts
* Understand printable resolution requirements (300 DPI preferred)
* Be able to generate/edit vector or layered artwork when needed
* Have experience with tools such as:
 * ChatGPT
 * Midjourney
 * DALL·E
 * Photoshop
 * Illustrator
 * Firefly
 * Canva Pro
 * Affinity Designer
 * Other AI-assisted design tools

⸻

Style Requirements

The style should be:

* Bright
* Fun
* Educational
* Premium-quality
* Similar to top international kids workbook brands
* Highly engaging for children
* Parent-friendly
* Print-friendly

⸻

South African Context

The content must include:

* South African animals
* Landmarks
* Cultures
* Languages
* Educational relevance to CAPS curriculum

⸻

Deliverables

The freelancer should be able to provide:

* High-resolution PNG files
* Editable source files
* PDF exports
* Consistent page templates
* Organized file structures

⸻

Bonus Skills (Highly Advantageous)

* Educational content experience
* CAPS curriculum understanding
* Illustration skills
* Character design consistency
* Prompt engineering expertise
* Experience creating children’s books or worksheets

⸻

To Apply

Please include:

1. Portfolio/examples of children’s educational work
2. AI-generated design examples
3. Tools/software you use
4. Your workflow for creating consistent educational packs
5. Your estimated turnaround time for a 16-page weekly pack
6. Your pricing structure

This is potentially a long-term project with ongoing weekly work.

full_description:
We are looking for a highly skilled Audio Engineer with a strong instrument-learning / music education background to join our team. This is a broad and hands-on role for someone who enjoys acting as a true “Swiss army knife” across audio, music technology, and applied AI. The role is primarily operational and execution-focused: you will work directly on creating, editing and delivering high-quality audio content and supporting a variety of music production projects at Tomplay. Beyond hands-on production work, you will also stay on top of the rapidly evolving AI landscape, researching and testing emerging tools and technologies in music and audio. Your goal will be to help Tomplay identify and leverage the best AI solutions available to continuously improve our content creation workflows and production capabilities.

You will be joining Tomplay’s music production team and your main responsibilities will include:

• Create, edit and deliver high-quality audio files and tracks across a wide range of music projects 
• Mix, edit and enhance audio files, refining balance, dynamics, and overall sound quality to deliver polished final productions
• Research, evaluate, test, and implement new AI tools and technologies to improve and support music and audio production workflows
• Work with sheet music files and notation software (e.g. Sibelius), making score adjustments and ensuring accurate alignment between scores and audio content
• Collaborate with creative and production teams to meet artistic and technical requirements

What we require from you: 
• Strong background in sound engineering, audio production, music technology or a related field
• Ability to read sheet music and strong knowledge of music theory. You play an instrument and have a genuine interest in music learning and music education
• Hands-on experience creating, editing, mixing and delivering high-quality audio productions using AI-powered tools for music creation, audio processing or content production
• Strong interest in AI applied to music and audio, with a genuine curiosity for emerging technologies and tools
• A classically trained background is a plus, but not required
• Experience with Sibelius and Melodyne is a plus, but not required
• Fluency in English 
• Excellent communication skills to coordinate with all stakeholders involved in a project
What we offer: 
• Flexibility to join on a part-time or full-time basis (to be discussed) 
• Fully remote position with flexible working arrangements 
• Work on a broad variety of hands-on projects across music, audio, and applied AI 
• A unique opportunity to stay at the forefront of innovation and work with the latest AI tools and technologies 
• Join one of the world’s leading music education and music technology companies, helping millions of musicians worldwide

We’re excited to hear from you!

full_description:
I am moving a single, fully structured PostgreSQL dataset (in the 1 TB – 10 TB range) into Snowflake and want the entire flow handled through Snowflake Openflow so that future incremental loads can piggy-back on the same orchestration. 

You will start by designing the Openflow pipeline, mapping every PostgreSQL schema, table, and index to its Snowflake equivalent, and putting in place the monitoring hooks Snowflake recommends for high-volume transfers. The goal is a like-for-like copy—no transformations—so row counts, constraints, and data types must match exactly once the job completes. 

Because of the data volume I need solid strategies for parallelisation, fault tolerance, and restartability. Please plan for a test run on a reduced slice, followed by a full push, and finish with a quick validation script so I have an auditable trail that the migration completed without loss. 

Deliverables 
• Openflow job definition(s) and any helper scripts or SQL files 
• Clear, step-by-step execution guide so I can rerun or extend the pipeline later 
• Post-migration validation report (row counts, failed row log, timing) 

I have admin-level access to both the source PostgreSQL instance and the target Snowflake account and can provide connection details as soon as we start. If you have completed similar high-volume migrations before, detail your approach and any relevant Snowflake optimisations you would bring in.

full_description:
Project Synopsis:
I need a refined, organic hook and receiver clasp system for a premium modular bracelet line (3.8 mm maximum diameter).
Key Requirements:

Hook: Elegant, slightly organic curved shape with a 2 mm ball at the end for a soft “click” lock.
Receiver: Designed to accept the hook smoothly, featuring a C-shaped cavity for the ball and an F-brace for security. Should create a gentle but firm locking feel.

Crimp Tube: Separate modular 7–8.5 mm long tube with ~3.2–3.3 mm inner diameter for leather/silk cord (with internal grip texture if possible).

Jump Ring / Connector: Small universal oval or teardrop jump ring (≈1.8–2.0 mm wire) for modularity.

Technical Constraints: Minimum wall thickness 0.6–0.7 mm (for lost-wax Argentium silver casting). Overall assembly must stay under 3.8 mm to allow beads to slide over.

Style: Clean, elegant, slightly mysterious — refined Art Deco / organic minimalism. No sharp edges, high-end jewelry feel.

Deliverables:

3D models (STEP + native format) of hook, receiver, crimp tube, and jump ring
Assembly views showing how they connect
Basic dimension drawings

full_description:
I run several OTT channels that regularly spin up new Android apps. For every new launch I need a fresh clone produced, fully branded, integrated with our existing streaming content and user-authentication modules, then signed and uploaded to Google Play under the correct package name.

This is continuous work, often with short notice, so I rely on someone who freelances full-time and can respond throughout the day as tasks appear. Each assignment typically involves:

• taking my base source, adjusting assets, colours, IDs and API keys 
• confirming streaming playback and login flows operate as on the original build 
• preparing screenshots, descriptions and release notes I supply 
• creating the release in Google Play Console, handling all store-compliance checks, then pushing the update or new listing live

Company Description

[login to view URL] is Africa's leading AI-powered background verification and primary source verification (PSV) company, headquartered in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia, with operations in 21 countries. We empower organizations, including HR teams, healthcare institutions, financial firms, and government bodies, to make informed hiring and compliance decisions with speed and confidence. Our mission focuses on simplifying credential verification processes to ensure professionals meet recognized licensing requirements, such as DHA, DOH, SCFHS, and MoH. We specialize in healthcare compliance and regulatory support, connecting local healthcare professionals with global career opportunities.



Role Description

This is a full-time, on-site role located in Zimbabwe for a Field Verification & Local Point of Contact. The selected candidate will perform field verification tasks, including visiting local agencies and organizations to gather and verify credential data. Responsibilities also include serving as the local point of contact, addressing client inquiries, troubleshooting issues, and supporting the coordination of verification processes. The individual will uphold professional standards while ensuring timely and accurate reporting.



Qualifications

Strong analytical skills to assess data accuracy and identify discrepancies
Excellent communication skills for client interactions and coordinating with local agencies
Proficiency in troubleshooting and problem-solving to resolve verification challenges
Customer service and client relationship management skills
Ability to provide training and guidance on processes as needed
Detail-oriented, self-disciplined, and capable of working independently
Familiarity with local regulatory and compliance standards is an advantage
Bachelor's degree preferred; relevant professional experience will be considered

full_description:
I want a seasoned reputation-management partner who can keep negative stories about my brand off page one of Google—and show me clear, measurable progress while doing it.

Here’s the rhythm I need:

Monitoring & Rapid Alerts 
I already have Google Alerts feeding mentions of our core keywords, but I expect you to layer in your own tracking stack, check results daily, and send a concise alert the moment any unfavourable page starts climbing. A quick snapshot of position changes and URL details is enough for me to make decisions fast.

Suppression & Promotion Strategy 
The primary weapon will be fresh, authoritative blog posts hosted either on our site or relevant third-party publications you secure. Once each piece is live, I want it supported by strategic link building on:

• High-authority blogs 
• News websites 

Supplementary social or digital-PR pushes are welcome if they help the positive assets index faster and collect backlinks naturally.

Transparency & Metrics 
Every week I expect a simple dashboard or sheet showing: the offending URLs, their current average positions, backlinks acquired to our positive assets, and any uplift in branded sentiment metrics you track. Typical timelines you’ve achieved for similar pushes—two weeks, four weeks, etc.—will help me set internal expectations.

Engagement Terms 
I’m flexible: propose an ongoing retainer or phased milestones, but make sure you outline the output I can expect at each checkpoint and how performance will be judged. Sustainability matters more than short-term tricks, so white-hat, resilient techniques only.

If this sounds like your wheelhouse, tell me how you’d roll out the first 30 days, what resources you’ll need from my side, and the concrete wins I should see by the end of month one.

full_description:
Project Title:

Global Construction Industry Research & Construction ERP Gap Analysis (Worldwide + India Focus)

Project Description:

We are seeking an experienced researcher/business analyst to conduct deep research on the global construction industry and existing construction ERP ecosystem. The objective is to identify why current ERPs fail, what challenges construction companies face while using them, missing features, adoption barriers, and opportunities to build a better ERP solution.

The research should cover major construction markets including India, UAE, Saudi Arabia, USA, UK, Australia, Singapore, Canada, Europe, China, Africa, and other growing regions.

The freelancer must conduct a detailed comparison study of existing construction ERPs, including:

Pricing, subscription, setup, implementation, and customization costs

Available modules and features

Target customer segments

Strengths and weaknesses

Customer complaints and reviews

Ease of use and implementation complexity

Hidden costs and total ownership cost


The research must identify:

Why ERP implementations fail

Why companies continue using Excel/manual systems despite ERP availability

Why employees avoid using ERP after implementation

Common pain points including usability, training, mobile limitations, integration issues, and complexity

Missing features users repeatedly request


The freelancer should determine what existing ERPs solve, where they fail, and where opportunities exist to build a better solution.

Country-wise analysis should include:

Compliance and tax requirements

Labour laws

Tender systems

Construction workflows

Local ERP customization needs


The project should also explore future opportunities such as AI-powered ERP, automation, predictive analytics, mobile-first solutions, BIM integration, IoT, and SME-focused ERP models.

Required Deliverables:

Strategic research report (PDF)

ERP comparison and pricing database

Pain point and missing feature analysis

Country-wise comparison study

Market gap and opportunity analysis

ERP roadmap and MVP recommendations

Supporting references and Excel/Sheets database


Expected Outcome: Identify ERP failures, adoption barriers, unmet industry needs, and opportunities to develop a more practical and competitive construction ERP solution.
